name: cuid

metadata.name: cuid
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# cuid.py 

Implementation of https://github.com/ericelliott/cuid in Python.

A `cuid` is a portable and sequentially-ordered unique identifier designed for
horizontal scalability and speed -- this version is ported from the reference
implementation in Javascript.

**NOTE**: Particularly if you have security concerns, the [`cuid` standard has been deprecated in favor of `cuid2`](https://github.com/paralleldrive/cuid2)!

Tested on CPython 2.7, 3.6-3.11 as well as PyPy & PyPy3.

Rough benchmarks on my machine (i7-8750H CPU @ 2.20GHz) using `setup.py bench`
(which times the creation of 1 million `cuid`s):

| Version                    | ns / cuid |
| -------------------------- | --------- |
| CPython 3.7.3              | 6095.257  |
| CPython 3.6.8              | 6846.050  |
| CPython 3.5.6              | 6604.012  |
| CPython 2.7.16             | 6913.681  |
| PyPy 7.1.1 (Python 2.7.13) | 326.344   |
| PyPy3 7.1.1 (Python 3.6.1) | 562.673   |

_(Note that timing the creation of fewer IDs changes the way PyPy runs the code, because of JIT warmup --
obviously creating this many IDs takes advantage of the warmed JIT)_

For now, this has no dependencies outside the standard library -- in time this may change, 
to provide better random numbers and / or performance.

methods.name: setup.bench.run
documentation: Ensure that several cuids can be generated per millisecond

class_variables.name: setup.bench.user_options
documentation: distutils complains if this is not here.

modules.name: cuid
documentation:
A `cuid` is a secure, portable, and sequentially-ordered identifier
designed for horizontal scalability and speed -- the reference implementation
is in Javascript at https://github.com/ericelliott/cuid

functions.name: cuid._to_base36
documentation:
Convert a positive integer to a base36 string.

Taken from Stack Overflow and modified.

```
param int number

integer to convert

rtype

str
```

functions.name: cuid._pad
documentation:
'Pad' a string with leading zeroes to fit the given size, truncating
if necessary.

```
param str string

string to pad

param int size

length of result

rtype

str
```

functions.name: cuid._random_block
documentation:
Generate a random string of `BLOCK_SIZE` length.

```
rtype

str
```

functions.name: cuid.get_process_fingerprint
documentation:
Extract a unique fingerprint for the current process, using a
combination of the process PID and the system's hostname.

```
rtype

str
```

methods.name: cuid.CuidGenerator.__init__
documentation: :param str fingerprint: process fingerprint to use

methods.name: cuid.CuidGenerator.counter
documentation:
Rolling counter that ensures same-machine and same-time
cuids don't collide.

```
return

counter value

rtype

int
```

methods.name: cuid.CuidGenerator.cuid
documentation:
Generate a full-length cuid as a string.

```
rtype

str
```

methods.name: cuid.CuidGenerator.slug
documentation:
Generate a short (7-character) cuid as a string.

While this is a convenient shorthand, this is much less likely
to be unique and should not be relied on. Prefer full-size
cuids where possible.

```
rtype

str
```
